_provenance.source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 077 (control/outbox.md block stamped 2026-07-15T23:10:50Z) + ideas/superbot-idle/event-fold-visibility-floor-2026-07-15.md @ idea-engine main 8aa71ba (landed via idea-engine PR #442, merged 2026-07-15T23:14:34Z by github-actions[bot], head cb9c2d9)
_provenance.ground_truth: COMMITTED external constants re-verified FIRSTHAND before this fixture was written, on a read-only clone of superbot-idle @ 884aeae9687742a389a2e2086a4cc930e5a4f3ee: docs/design/timed-events-scoping.md section 2 seventh-factor fold + graduation identity (code block verbatim), section 1a proportionality promise, section 5 SE-2 'credited exactly the window bonus', section 3 'state_version stays 2', section 6 'nothing in this doc is built'; idle_engine/engine.py six-factor fold // 100_000_000 with count INSIDE the single floor ('The floor division happens ONCE per generator per second'); idle_engine/economy.py UPGRADE_EFFECT_PERCENT=25, PRESTIGE_BONUS_PERCENT=10, MILESTONE_BONUS_PERCENT=5, 9 milestone rungs (3x3 threshold tuples); themes/*.yaml base_rate multiset {1: 18, 5: 17}, zero balance blocks, 18 packs; idle_engine/state.py rate_multiplier_pct bounds 90..110. All verbatim, zero harvest anomalies. The sim itself is hermetic: it reads ONLY this fixtures.json
_provenance.note: values copied verbatim from the registration; the runner re-derives everything from scratch and must not trust the anchors — a mismatch is a first-class anomaly

fold.committed_event_fold: rate_event = base_rate * count * upgrade_pct * prestige_pct * milestone_pct * theme_pct * event_pct // 10_000_000_000 (timed-events-scoping.md section 2, code block verbatim; count and event pct INSIDE the single floor, pinned by the doc's code block and engine.py's shipped precedent)
fold.committed_today_fold: rate_today = base_rate * count * upgrade_pct * prestige_pct * milestone_pct * theme_pct // 100_000_000 (engine.py production_per_second, shipped)
fold.graduation_identity: (x * 100) // 10_000_000_000 == x // 100_000_000 asserted on EVERY lattice cell (F1)

lattice.iteration_order: itertools.product order over (base, count, L, u, m, theme)
lattice.cap_note: caps count<=25, L<=12, u<=10 are the axis-cap convention (NULL axis ii); R1/R3 cap-free by construction; census rows cap-relative and disclosed

repair_arms.a_min_delta: V038's registered min-visible-delta fallback: rate_event' = max(rate_event, rate_today + 1) during a window

b_milli_ledger.law: milli rates: R0m = x*1000 // 10^8, REm = x*E*1000 // 10^10; price = save v3 + golden-corpus same-PR policy + economy re-registration (breaks the doc's section 3 promise)

c_rate_floor.law: event proportionality promised only at rate_today >= R_star; price = the start state stays dead (concedes Horn 2's 'for idlers and actives alike')

f3_census_anchors.staircase_envelope: floor(R*E/100) <= RE <= ceil((R+1)*E/100) - 1 on every alive cell, BOTH edges attained at every grid E

pencil_worlds.calendar_law: piecewise closed-form credit == 1-second tick loop, byte-equal, on four lattice cells x all five grid multipliers, including the degenerate all-zero-delta trace
pencil_worlds.calendar_cells_note: VACANCY: the registration pins 'four lattice cells' by COUNT only — the four cells are sim-chosen (canonical, jackpot witness, one-upgrade neighbour, lattice max) and disclosed here; the degenerate all-zero-delta trace is the canonical cell at x1.10

typed_must_equal_contacts.contact_2: arm (a) min-delta overshoot on the canonical cell at x1.10 == the x1.10 lattice jackpot ratio, both exactly 20/11
typed_must_equal_contacts.contact_3: the x1.10 dead census (Arm A integer) == the independent Fraction-classifier count (Arm B)

arm_r.draw_order_grammar: REGISTERED (the V089 lesson, applied by the drafter): one random.Random(seed) per trace set; per trace exactly three draws in this order: (1) cell index uniform over the alive-cell list in lattice iteration order (base, count, L, u, m, theme — itertools.product order), (2) E uniform over the grid tuple, (3) window length W uniform integer in 600..86400 s
arm_r.seed_gap_disclosed: 20261684-689 is the disclosed in-flight buffer; 20261680-683 are P076/V089's registered set

controls.theme_100_sublattice: NULL axis (i): committed-packs sub-lattice (theme = 100 only) — pre-checked non-flipping at drafting (the canonical cell is a theme-100 cell and is dead at four grid multipliers); the verdict re-derives it
controls.cap_bump: NULL axis (ii): count/L/u caps moved up one notch (26/13/11) must not flip R1/R3 — R1 is cap-free by construction
controls.e_grid_convention: NULL axis (iii): T1's all-E sweep is grid-free; census rows are per-E findings, never aggregated
controls.e100_neutrality: F5: at E = 100 the event delta is identically 0 on every alive cell (the graduation identity restated)
controls.alt_fold_named_control: VACANCY (sim-chosen value, control registered by NAME only): the alternative fold reading (count OUTSIDE the single floor: rate = count * (per-unit fold)) is carried as a named REPORTING-ONLY control — a different fold reading would dissolve T1; the committed reading (count inside) is pinned by the doc's code block and engine.py

decision_rule.REJECT: R1 (dead band): canonical-cell event delta = 0 for all 99 integer E in 101..199 AND first paying E = 200 exactly AND the one-upgrade neighbour still dead at E = 150. AND R2 (census + jackpot): alive-cell dead censuses exactly (4,151 / 675 / 56 / 14 / 0) AND the x1.10 realized/nominal maximum exactly 20/11 on a rate-1 witness AND the staircase envelope holds with both edges attained at every grid E. AND R3 (repair fork): arm (a) overshoot = 20/11 exactly AND arm (b) milli-census = 0 with the >= 99 certificate AND arm (c) deviation bound = 1/22 exactly with zero dead cells at rate >= 20.
decision_rule.INVALID: any F1-F6 gate failure (controls misbehaving — report, no ruling)
decision_rule.APPROVE: every grid E has dead census 0 AND realized/nominal in [20/21, 21/20] on all alive cells — satisfied by the arm-(b) milli-granular engine, mutually exclusive with REJECT by arithmetic
decision_rule.NULL: anything else; pre-registered axes: (i) theme-100 committed-packs sub-lattice, (ii) axis-cap convention, (iii) E-grid convention — every NULL a finalized citable finding with exact censuses attached, never a re-run request

margin_ledger_registered_cells.first_paying_e_contact: E = 200 is a registered margin-0 contact (canonical rate 1 -> 2, the minimal positive delta)
margin_ledger_registered_cells.dead_band_saturated: 99/99 dead is a saturated cell (every E in the sane band — no room above)
margin_ledger_registered_cells.e200_census_zero_cell: the x2.00 dead census 0 is a zero cell, certified by floor(2r) >= 2*floor(r) > floor(r) for r >= 1
margin_ledger_registered_cells.jackpot_equality: the x1.10 maximum realized/nominal is EXACTLY 20/11 (equality, not clearance)
margin_ledger_registered_cells.overshoot_equals_jackpot: arm (a) overshoot == jackpot ratio, both 20/11 (typed contact 2)
margin_ledger_registered_cells.deviation_equality: arm (c) deviation bound is EXACTLY 1/22, attained at x1.10 on a rate-20 cell
margin_ledger_registered_cells.milli_minimum: arm (b) canonical milli-delta EXACTLY 100; lattice minimum a clearance >= the certified 99
margin_ledger_registered_cells.payers_at_x2_equals_alive: payers-at-x2.00 == alive (typed contact 4; saturated — every alive cell pays)

perception_boundary: V038's committed definition imported verbatim: 'felt = integer rate delta > 0' — a fractional-rate UI would change the question and the engine exposes none
value_boundary: the doc registers NO bonus values by design — every theorem is per-E, T1 quantifies over the whole sane band 101..199, and no ruling here proposes or blesses a bonus value
expected_landing_disclosed: REJECT on R1 AND R2 AND R3 at the drafter's exact values (26/26 drafting checks PASS, exit 0, ~11 s); the sim re-derives everything from scratch and must not trust these

battery.byte_identity: stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs (Arms A/B seedless exact integer/Fraction arithmetic, platform-independent; Arm R pinned to the stated CPython minor)
battery.anomaly_census_convention: structured by disclosure coverage (V087-V089): compared-and-matched / compared-and-mismatched / not-disclosed(vacant); mismatches are first-class anomalies
